summary: Excellent audience‑recorded tape of a legendary Richmond ’85 show – crystal‑clear Oade “audience soundboard” captures Jerry’s soaring ballads and a fire‑hot second set.
review: The Oade brothers’ audience capture is praised as crisp, upfront and almost as clean as a board mix, letting listeners hear every nuance from the stage. Musically the concert is hailed for an all‑Jerry night – blistering High Time solo, heartfelt Comes A Time, soaring She Belongs To Me, a thunderous Gloria and a legendary Lost Sailor/​Saint sequence – with only a few mixed feelings about “He’s Gone.”

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance and recording; the show is repeatedly called one of ’85’s best, and the Oade audience source is described as “excellent” with no serious technical flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note mic placement close to Bill Mickey’s cymbals, crystal‑clear mix, minimal crowd bleed, no dropout or distortion – essentially a high‑fidelity “audience soundboard.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: Unusual but exciting ordering – He’s Gone followed by Lost Sailor, then a surprise Spoonful/Comes A Time sequence before drums; the second set stretches beyond the typical three‑song pre‑drums block and keeps the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly highlighted for his vocal power and an unforgettable solo on High Time; described as “all Jerry” with a hot guitar tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: A few reviewers note Phil was restless, trying to pick up the pace while Jerry lingered; one mentions Phil’s “louder!” moment in the rafters.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir gets kudos for his “spelling lesson” on Gloria and solid rhythm work on Spoonful.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) is praised for his heavy presence, harmonies and overall contribution; several reviewers wish they could have seen him live.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart are lauded for tight playing; reviewers comment the mics were “a foot from” their cymbals giving a vivid drum sound.
